description: Governed HIVE skill catalogue metadata. Entries enrich imported D1/R2 skill records; fallback rules prevent blank descriptions for registry skills that arrive without local metadata.

defaults.description: Supports a governed HIVE operational task using registry metadata and review-gated planning.
defaults.when_to_use: Use when a request matches the skill title, tags, repository or lane metadata.

fallback_rules.description: Analyses logs, failed checks and deployment signals to identify the smallest safe fix.
Reviews repository evidence and prepares a bounded, testable change plan.
Checks security-sensitive configuration, access boundaries and approval gates before changes proceed.
Works with configured Cloudflare storage lanes using bounded reads, writes only where explicitly allowed.
Improves skill, file or context retrieval using configured index metadata and safe fallback paths.
Reviews or prepares brand-aware content while keeping QA recommendations future-facing.
Reviews podcast transcript, metadata or publishing evidence for QA and amplification planning.
Reviews eBook catalogue, keyword or positioning evidence without inventing marketplace data.
Checks production-readiness evidence and turns findings into a controlled improvement plan.

items.title: CI/log analysis
items.description: Analyses CI logs, runtime logs and deployment output to find the first meaningful failure and the safest next fix.
items.when_to_use: Use for GitHub Actions, Koyeb, Cloudflare Pages or runtime diagnostic bundles.

items.title: Repo/debug bundle
items.description: Inspects repository ZIPs, failing tests and deployment artefacts to produce a minimal patch plan.
items.when_to_use: Use when a repo upload, stack trace or failing build needs grounded triage.

items.title: Audit report review
items.description: Summarises audit bundles, highlights production risks and turns evidence into a review-gated improvement path.
items.when_to_use: Use for RAMS/AIMS/HIVE audit reports, readiness checks and QA evidence packs.

items.title: Social content QA
items.description: Reviews social, RSS and short-form content for brand fit, clarity and future QA improvements.
items.when_to_use: Use for captions, hooks, platform posts, RSS rewrites and brand-safety checks.

items.title: Podcast episode review
items.description: Reviews podcast transcripts, episode metadata and RSS wording for QA and amplification opportunities.
items.when_to_use: Use for podcast transcript reviews, episode pages, show notes and podcast SEO planning.

items.title: eBook keyword review
items.description: Reviews eBook keyword notes, catalogue positioning and niche-gap evidence without inventing market data.
items.when_to_use: Use for KDP keyword council work, catalogue planning and eBook positioning checks.

items.title: Skill registry integrity
items.description: Checks shared skill records for missing fields, duplicate identifiers and taxonomy drift before routing work.
items.when_to_use: Use when the skill catalogue looks stale, duplicated or under-described.

items.title: Repository hygiene
items.description: Finds duplicate, generated or stale files and returns a reviewable deletion manifest without deleting automatically.
items.when_to_use: Use before repo cleanup commits or when old patch notes and generated artefacts clutter production branches.

items.title: Production readiness
items.description: Validates production-critical configuration, dependency gates and operator safety before deployment.
items.when_to_use: Use before Koyeb deployment or after env, auth, database, storage or model-routing changes.

items.title: R2 lane review
items.description: Reviews configured R2 lanes, bucket mappings and public-base URLs for safe read/write behaviour.
items.when_to_use: Use when adding buckets, checking file access or debugging storage-lane behaviour.

items.title: Model routing review
items.description: Checks OpenRouter model defaults, fallback policy and stream behaviour against cost and reliability goals.
items.when_to_use: Use when replies are slow, truncated, empty or routed through unsuitable models.
